During the Cold War, several leaders held power in different countries. In the United States, Presidents Truman, Eisenhower, Kennedy, Johnson, Nixon, Ford, Carter, Reagan, and Bush were in charge at various times. In the Soviet Union, leaders such as Stalin, Khrushchev, Brezhnev, Andropov, Chernenko, and Gorbachev held power. These leaders shaped the political and military strategies of their nations during this tense period.
